text.title: interface-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for interface-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Static spec drift: hand-written Markdown interface specs decay the moment code ships because they are disconnected from the implementation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generate specs from OpenAPI/AsyncAPI/Protobuf source of truth or sync via oasdiff in CI. Hand-typed specs as primary artifacts guarantee drift within days of the first deploy.

failure-mode.description: Volume and SLA fields filled with confident estimates pre-launch: "expected: 10,000 requests/day".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pre-launch volume estimates are guesses with order-of-magnitude error. Mark fields "tbd" until load-test data exists. Confident fictions produce wrong capacity planning decisions.

failure-mode.description: Async event flows (Kafka, webhooks, NATS) documented using the REST interface template.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: REST templates cannot represent fan-out direction, consumer-side retry semantics, or per-key ordering guarantees. Use AsyncAPI 3.0 for all event-driven interfaces.

failure-mode.description: Error codes auto-completed with the standard HTTP set even when the API returns only 3 of them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hallucinated error codes mislead client developers who write error-handling for codes that never fire. Validate against captured traffic; document only codes that the API actually emits.

failure-mode.description: Auth fields (OAuth scopes, API key header location) generated from the agent's priors without citing the vendor docs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auth fields are the highest-risk spec fields: wrong scopes produce authorization failures; wrong header names produce 401 errors that look like network issues. Require source-cited auth fields; reject auth rows without evidence URLs.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tools and agents copy-paste payload examples into tests. Placeholders in examples produce tests that assert against literal "[value]" strings instead of real data. Examples must be literal.

failure-mode.description: Cross-team interfaces failing at the human boundary: the spec is correct but the other team owns auth rotation and breaks it without notice.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Technical specs cannot solve organizational coordination. Pair IF-XXX specs with named contacts and a change-notification protocol. oasdiff in CI catches changes automatically; human contacts catch intentional breaking changes before they deploy.

failure-mode.description: Agents hallucinate field types when the source is ambiguous.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a "field types must come from sample payload or OpenAPI, otherwise mark as `unknown`" rule in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: Error code tables get auto-completed with the standard HTTP set even when the API only returns three of them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validate against captured traffic. Every code must be verified against live response.

failure-mode.description: Direction labels flip easily ("we provide" vs "we consume").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Anchor the prompt to a fixed perspective (always our system as subject).

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Split event-driven interfaces into a separate AsyncAPI pass, do not reuse the REST template.

failure-mode.description: Auth fields (OAuth scopes, API key locations) are routinely wrong.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to cite the line in vendor docs that defines them.

failure-mode.description: Volume/SLA numbers must be marked `tbd` until load test data exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents will otherwise emit confident fictions that produce wrong capacity planning.

failure-mode.description: When generating contract tests from a spec, tests pass against mock but fail on live system.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run tests against both the live system and the mock; a green mock + red live = spec drift, not test bug.
